wrapUp: Matchday twelve centres on qualification deciders in Group L, where England and Ghana meet in Boston with both sides on three points and Thomas Tuchel reporting a fully fit squad including Bukayo Saka, while Thomas Partey's visa saga ends with clearance to face his former employers after missing the Panama opener in Canada. Scotland's path through Group C runs via a narrow defeat to Brazil in Miami, where Neymar is back in training but Raphinha is ruled out and Carlo Ancelotti may turn to Rayan on the right. Germany advance without Nico Schlotterbeck, Luka Modric earns his 200th Croatia cap in a must-win Panama fixture, and Japan's record-breaking win has reshaped the Netherlands' race at the top of Group F. Each story below links to the specific report it is drawn from; squad and fitness details reflect the latest available reporting as of 23 June 2026.

sections.heading: Injuries and fitness doubts
sections.description: Players ruled out, late replacements and day-to-day concerns that could change starting line-ups and squad depth.

stories.headline: Rice fit to face Ghana despite managed hamstring neural pain
stories.summary: Declan Rice declared himself "fit and raring to go" for England's Group L meeting with Ghana on 23 June after Thomas Tuchel substituted him as a precaution in the 72nd minute of the 4-2 win over Croatia. Rice told ITV he has been managing neural pain in his hamstring since Christmas, a complaint Arsenal handled behind the scenes during their title run-in, and described Tuchel's decision as smart given the injury risk in the closing stages of matches. With Tuchel now confirming everyone trained and is available in Boston, Rice's availability as midfield anchor remains central to England's push for an early knockout berth.

stories.headline: Tuchel confirms Saka pain-free and available against Ghana
stories.summary: Thomas Tuchel said Bukayo Saka feels no more pain from the Achilles tendinitis that kept him on the bench against Croatia, with the Arsenal winger completing both training sessions at full intensity before England's Group L fixture with Ghana on 23 June. Tuchel told reporters that everyone in the squad trained and is available, though he declined to say whether Saka or Noni Madueke will start on the right wing. A win in Boston would secure England's place in the Round of 32 with a game to spare, and Saka's return to full fitness gives Tuchel a proven wide option off the bench or in the starting XI.

stories.headline: Neymar completes first full training session ahead of Scotland
stories.summary: Neymar completed his first full training session with Brazil on 21 June after missing the draws with Morocco and the 3-0 win over Haiti with a grade-two calf strain sustained playing for Santos on 17 May. Carlo Ancelotti has said the 34-year-old will be included in the matchday squad for the Group C finale against Scotland on 24 June in Miami, potentially his first international appearance since October 2023. With Raphinha ruled out of that fixture, Neymar's return would give Brazil a proven outlet in attack before the knockout stage, though how many minutes he can manage after a month out remains to be seen.

stories.headline: Raphinha ruled out of Scotland match with hamstring injury
stories.summary: Raphinha will miss Brazil's Group C decider against Scotland on 24 June after scans confirmed a hamstring injury sustained in the first half of the 3-0 win over Haiti on 19 June. The CBF said the Barcelona forward will undergo intensive treatment with the aim of returning for a possible last-16 fixture in early July, but Lucas Paqueta acknowledged the squad are losing a key wide outlet for the Scotland match. Raphinha had started both group games and his absence opens a starting berth that Neymar is now expected to fill from the bench, though Brazil already face the tournament without Rodrygo, Estêvão and Wesley.

stories.headline: Doku misses Belgium's goalless draw with Iran through illness
stories.summary: Jérémy Doku was unavailable for Belgium's 0-0 draw with Iran on 21 June in Los Angeles because of illness, depriving Rudi Garcia's side of their most direct wide outlet in a match they dominated without finding a breakthrough. Thibaut Courtois and Alireza Beiranvand produced several fine saves at either end before Nathan Ngoy was sent off in the 66th minute, yet Iran held on with ten men to leave both teams on two points in Group G. Doku's availability for Belgium's final group fixture will matter in a section where Egypt and New Zealand also remain in contention.

stories.headline: Schlotterbeck ruled out for remainder of World Cup
stories.summary: Nico Schlotterbeck has been ruled out of the rest of the World Cup after scans on 21 June confirmed a torn medial ligament in his left ankle, sustained during Germany's 2-1 win over Ivory Coast on 20 June in Toronto. The Borussia Dortmund centre-back played on until half-time before Antonio Rüdiger replaced him, and Julian Nagelsmann said Germany will miss his build-up play from the back as they head into the knockouts despite already topping Group E. Rüdiger is expected to partner Jonathan Tah in the last 32, with Waldemar Anton and Malick Thiaw as further options at centre-back.

stories.headline: Pulisic expected fit for Turkiye but Pochettino may rest captain
stories.summary: Christian Pulisic missed the 2-0 win over Australia on 19 June after training separately all week with a left calf problem sustained during the 4-1 opener against Paraguay, but reports suggest he should be fit for the Group D finale against Turkiye on 25 June. With the co-hosts already confirmed as group winners, Mauricio Pochettino may choose not to risk his captain in a dead rubber, prioritising full sharpness for the Round of 32 in Santa Clara on 1 July. Ricardo Pepi impressed as a starter in Seattle, giving Pochettino a credible alternative if Pulisic is held back for the knockouts.

sections.heading: Squad selection and tactical reshuffles
sections.description: Omissions, late call-ups and manager decisions that reveal how teams plan to navigate the expanded format.

stories.headline: Ancelotti set to pick Rayan as Raphinha's replacement against Scotland
stories.summary: Carlo Ancelotti must reshuffle Brazil's attack for the Group C finale against Scotland on 24 June after Raphinha's hamstring injury against Haiti, with Bournemouth teenager Rayan the most likely starter on the right flank. Ancelotti has confirmed Neymar "will be available" after his calf recovery but the 34-year-old is expected to begin on the bench, leaving Matheus Cunha and Vinicius Junior as the settled central and left outlets after Cunha's brace against Haiti. With Rodrygo, Estêvão and Wesley already absent, Brazil's reduced wide depth makes Ancelotti's selection between Rayan and a possible Neymar cameo pivotal before the knockouts.

stories.headline: Renard's Tunisia bow ends in 4-0 defeat and group exit
stories.summary: Hervé Renard's first match in charge of Tunisia ended in a 4-0 defeat to Japan on 21 June in Monterrey, eliminating the North Africans from Group F after just two matches. Ayase Ueda scored twice and Daichi Kamada opened the scoring inside four minutes in the 1,000th World Cup finals match, leaving Renard's side bottom of the group with zero points. The Frenchman was parachuted in after Sabri Lamouchi's sacking following a 5-1 opening loss to Sweden, but the mid-tournament reshuffle could not arrest a campaign already defined by heavy defeats.

sections.heading: Disruption and preparation issues
sections.description: Travel, visa and logistical problems that could affect match readiness rather than just headlines.

stories.headline: Partey cleared for US entry after Canada visa refusal
stories.summary: Thomas Partey missed Ghana's 1-0 win over Panama on 17 June because Canada refused his visa after he failed to disclose pending UK criminal charges on his application, but the midfielder has been admitted to the United States and says he is ready to face England on 23 June. US Customs and Border Protection confirmed Partey was issued a visa and had not been convicted of a crime, while FIFA noted he could not travel from Boston to Toronto for the Panama fixture. Partey's return strengthens Carlos Queiroz's midfield against an England side he spent six years facing at Arsenal, though goalkeeper Lawrence Ati-Zigi's groin issue from the opener remains a separate fitness doubt.

sections.heading: Analysis and tournament outlook
sections.description: Deeper previews on favourites, group dynamics and how the 48-team format changes the path to the final.

stories.headline: England and Ghana meet with knockout place in sight
stories.summary: England can book a Round of 32 berth with a game to spare if they beat Ghana on 23 June in Boston, after Harry Kane, Jude Bellingham and Marcus Rashford fired the Three Lions to a 4-2 opening win over Croatia. Ghana arrive on three points too after Caleb Yirenkyi's stoppage-time winner against Panama, their first World Cup victory since 2010, and a second successive win would secure their best tournament run since the 2010 quarter-finals. Thomas Tuchel may reshuffle central defence after conceding twice against Croatia, while Ghana could start Thomas Partey in midfield for the first time this tournament.

stories.headline: Scotland can advance via best-third route against Brazil
stories.summary: Scotland face Brazil on 24 June in Miami needing at most a narrow defeat to keep alive hopes of a first-ever knockout appearance via the best-third-placed formula, having taken three points from a win over Haiti and a 1-0 loss to Morocco. Steve Clarke's side improved after the break against the Atlas Lions but were undone by Ismael Saibari's 71-second opener, and John McGinn acknowledged Morocco "got away with one" in Boston. Brazil lead Group C on four points and can secure top spot with a convincing win, though their draws with Morocco and laboured Haiti victory have raised questions about whether Ancelotti's side have peaked yet.

stories.headline: Modric set for 200th cap as Croatia chase Panama redemption
stories.summary: Luka Modric will earn his 200th Croatia cap against Panama on 23 June in Toronto, with Zlatko Dalic's side under pressure after a 4-2 opening defeat to England left them needing points before a closing fixture with Ghana. Dalic admitted Croatia "gifted England a lot" defensively and demanded his favourites "act like it" against Panama, while Mateo Kovacic said the squad thrive when backs are against the wall as in 2018 and 2022. Modric's milestone adds emotional weight to a must-win evening for a team that reached the 2018 final and finished third four years ago.

stories.headline: Japan's record win eliminates Tunisia in 1,000th World Cup match
stories.summary: Japan beat Tunisia 4-0 on 21 June in the 1,000th match in World Cup history, matching the Netherlands' statement result from earlier in the day to move level on four points at the top of Group F. Daichi Kamada's fourth-minute opener was the fastest goal by a Japanese player at a World Cup, Ayase Ueda scored twice and Junya Ito added a third as Hajime Moriyasu's side posted the largest victory by any nation in tournament history. Tunisia's elimination leaves Japan and the Netherlands fighting for top spot before the closing fixtures against Sweden and Tunisia respectively on 25 June.

stories.headline: Yamal and Oyarzabal fire Spain to first Group H win
stories.summary: Lamine Yamal scored on his first World Cup start and Mikel Oyarzabal added two more as Spain beat Saudi Arabia 4-0 on 21 June in Atlanta, rebounding from the shock goalless draw with Cape Verde in their opener. Luis de la Fuente's side were three goals ahead before the first hydration break, with Hassan Altambakti's own goal completing the scoring after half-time. Spain move top of Group H on four points and need only a point against Uruguay on 26 June to secure a Round of 32 berth, while Yamal's return eases concerns over the hamstring issue that had kept him out of the Cape Verde match.

stories.headline: Gakpo and Brobbey doubles send Netherlands top of Group F
stories.summary: Brian Brobbey and Cody Gakpo each scored twice as the Netherlands beat Sweden 5-1 on 20 June in Houston to leapfrog their opponents into first place in Group F. Brobbey struck twice inside 17 minutes before Gakpo added a quickfire double after the break, with substitute Crysencio Summerville sealing a fifth in the 89th minute after Anthony Elanga had briefly reduced the arrears. The Oranje sit top on four points from two games, setting up closing group fixtures against Tunisia and a Japan-Sweden decider that will determine who tops the section on 25 June.
